# Abstract
We measure quantum coherence in superconducting qubits under variable noise. The experiment quantifies decoherence time scales.

# Introduction
Decoherence limits the performance of quantum processors. Characterizing noise is essential for error mitigation.

# Methods
Qubits were driven with Ramsey sequences across temperature settings. Noise spectra were estimated from phase variance.

# Results
Coherence time improved by 18 percent after filtering low frequency noise. The dominant noise source shifted with temperature.

Figure 1. Ramsey fringe decay across noise settings.

# Discussion
Filtering reduced dephasing but introduced control overhead. Results inform noise aware gate scheduling.

Table 1. Coherence times under each experimental condition.

# Conclusion
Noise characterization enables targeted mitigation in superconducting qubits. Future work will integrate adaptive filtering.

# References
[1] M. Physicist. Noise in superconducting qubits. 2015.
[2] N. Physicist. Decoherence mitigation methods. 2018.
